twitter_thread: I've seen 75% of 'discounted' home gyms break within a year. The other 25% were just overpriced to begin with.
Adjustable dumbbells like Bowflex can last 5+ years. Kettlebells from Rogue Fitness can go 10+.
The 'home gym bundle' from Walmart includes a $20 bench and $10 weights. Don't waste $100 on it.
Refurbished equipment from FitnessBlowout can be 30-50% cheaper than new gear.
Prime Day 'exclusives' like the $200 Peloton bike are often $50 cheaper on other sites within a week.
What's the best fitness deal you've scored, and how's the equipment held up?
Last year's model of the NordicTrack treadmill can be $300 cheaper than the new one.
pack.linkedin: A 50% discount on home gym equipment can be misleading, as 75% of these deals are on low-end gear that will break within a year. Instead, look for discounts on adjustable dumbbells like Bowflex or kettlebells from Rogue Fitness, which can last 5-10 years. Avoid bundles that include flimsy benches and cheap weights, and consider buying separate, quality pieces. You can also search for refurbished equipment from sites like FitnessBlowout for 30-50% off. What's been your experience with fitness deals, and what do you look for when making a purchase?
tiktok_hooks: 75% of home gyms are junk, here's why
Don't overpay for a Peloton, wait a week
The secret to buying quality fitness gear
pack.reddit_headline: Is it worth saving $100 on a home gym that'll break in 6 months, or should you splurge on quality equipment?
